title: Unlimited ERC20 Approval to Floor Contract
reason: This is the intended trust boundary between the periphery and the core `floor` contract. "If the trusted core is compromised" is not a distinct vulnerability in the periphery.

title: Unlimited ERC721 Approval to Floor Contract
reason: This approval is required for the periphery's normal fragment flow and only empowers the trusted `floor` contract. It is not a standalone exploit path.

title: No Access Control on extsload - Full Storage Exposure
reason: `IFlooring` itself intentionally exposes `extsload` as an external view utility. `FloorGetter` only wraps that public interface in typed getters and does not create a new write or theft primitive.

title: Delegatecall in Multicall Allows Contract Hijacking
reason: `multicall` delegatecalls only into `address(this)`, i.e. `FloorGetter`'s own functions. It does not execute attacker-supplied bytecode, and `FloorGetter` exposes no state-mutating external methods to hijack storage.

title: Missing Reentrancy Guards
reason: No concrete exploit path was identified. The periphery keeps almost no mutable state, and the cited external calls either revert atomically on failure or target trusted immutable dependencies.

title: No Deadline Validation in Swap Execution
reason: The Universal Router already enforces the deadline; its interface explicitly states that `execute` reverts when the deadline has passed.

title: Missing Input Size Limits - DoS Vector
reason: Unbounded arrays here only risk the caller exhausting their own gas and reverting their own transaction. They do not create a persistent or permissionless protocol-wide DoS condition.

title: Unchecked Permit2 Permit Results
reason: Permit2 `permit` and `permitTransferFrom` do not return booleans. They revert on failure, so there is no ignored success value.

title: No Signature Expiration in Permit2 Signature Transfer
reason: Permit2 signature-transfer structs include `deadline`, and Permit2 also uses nonce-based replay protection. The claimed indefinite replay issue is not supported by the interface.

title: Inconsistent Validation in Batch Transfer
reason: Supplying malformed transfer parameters only misconfigures the caller's own transaction. No protocol-level exploit or third-party harm follows from the cited code.

title: Missing Emit on Ownership Transfer
reason: `setOwner` does emit `OwnerUpdated`, and the broader complaint is about observability rather than a security vulnerability.

title: Incorrect Balance Check in _executeSwap
reason: A failed router call reverts the whole transaction, so ETH is not stranded by swap failure itself. The real issue is successful refunds and residual pooled balances, which is already captured in F-001.
